description: Fix the three black-screen pages (/ai-care-plans, /consent-manager, /digital-twin) and add a global React error boundary to prevent any future black screens.
steps: Step 1: Fix frontend/src/pages/ResidentDigitalTwin.tsx - The backend controller (digitalTwin.controller.ts) queries 'engagement_level' and 'logged_at' columns from wellbeing_logs. If migration 014 hasn't been applied, these queries fail with 500 errors. The frontend page has NO error handling for failed queries - if the initial residents query fails, `residents?.map(...)` is fine (undefined), but when a resident is selected and the twin/timeline queries fail, the component may try to render `twin.personal.name` etc. on undefined data. Fix: Add error state handling to the useQuery calls. Show user-friendly error messages when API calls fail. Add null safety for all data access (twin?.personal?.name etc.).

Step 2: Fix frontend/src/pages/ConsentManager.tsx - This page has NO error state handling. If the API call to `/consents/expiring/all` or `/consents/:residentId` fails (500 error from missing table or column), the unhandled error could crash the component. Fix: Add error state handling for all useQuery calls. Show user-friendly error messages. Ensure the expiring tab shows a graceful message when API fails.

Step 3: Fix frontend/src/pages/AiCarePlanWriter.tsx - The `plans` query to `/ai/care-plans` has no error handling in the UI for the GET query (only the mutation has error display). If the plans list query fails, `plans?.map(...)` returns undefined which is fine, BUT if `selectedPlan.content` is a JSON string instead of an object (JSONB double-serialization issue), accessing `selectedPlan.content.cqc_domains` would throw. Fix: Add a safe content parser that handles both string and object content. Add error state displays for all queries.

Step 4: Fix backend/src/controllers/digitalTwin.controller.ts - The getDigitalTwin function queries `engagement_level, logged_at` from wellbeing_logs. The getTimeline function also queries engagement_level. If migration 014 columns don't exist, these queries crash. Fix: Make the queries resilient - catch errors from these optional queries and return empty arrays instead of crashing. Also change 'engagement_level' to use a conditional check, or just select all columns with SELECT * and pick what's needed.

Step 5: Create frontend/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx - Create a React class component error boundary that catches rendering errors and displays a friendly error message with a 'Try Again' button instead of a black screen. Style it consistently with the app design (card-based, using CSS vars).

Step 6: Wrap the Routes in frontend/src/App.tsx with the new ErrorBoundary component - Import ErrorBoundary and wrap the <Routes> section so any page crash shows the error UI instead of a blank screen.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend builds successfully with `cd frontend && npm run build`
Backend builds successfully with `cd backend && npm run build`
ResidentDigitalTwin.tsx displays error messages when API calls fail instead of crashing
ConsentManager.tsx displays error messages when API calls fail instead of crashing
AiCarePlanWriter.tsx safely handles both string and object content fields, and shows errors for failed queries
digitalTwin.controller.ts gracefully handles missing columns by catching query errors for optional data
App.tsx wraps Routes with ErrorBoundary to catch any rendering errors globally
